title: Dow Jones And U.S. Stock Market Intraday Outlook - The Tech Honeymoon Is Over... Right Before Earnings?
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Nasdaq leads US benchmarks to the downside as earnings approach; Dow Jones holds strong between 49,000 and 49,500; S&P 500 technical levels remain critical.
excerpt: Nasdaq leads US benchmarks to the downside as earnings approach; Dow Jones holds strong between 49,000 and 49,500; S&P 500 technical levels remain critical.

content.introduction: Nasdaq leads US benchmarks to the downside as earnings approach; Dow Jones holds strong between 49,000 and 49,500; S&P 500 technical levels remain critical.
body: – Dow Jones and US Stock Market Intraday Outlook. * Nasdaq leads US Benchmarks to the downside and traders are now unrolling risk heavily ahead of the key earnings. * Exploring Technical Levels for the Dow Jones, Nasdaq and S&P 500. The Nasdaq is currently leading US Stock benchmarks to the downside, as traders scramble to unroll risk ahead of the most critical corporate Mega Cap earnings stretch of the year. ***Let's dive into intraday charts and trading levels for the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Nasdaq Composite, and S&P 500.***. ### Dow Jones 2H Chart and Trading Levels. Dow Jones (CFD) 2H Chart – April 28, 2026 – Source: TradingView. The Dow is still holding its **super-strong 500 point range between 49,000 and 49,500** – Keep these levels in check for the breakout trading. **Dow Jones technical levels for trading:**. * 49,900 to 50,000 Resistance and Early 2026 Highs. ### S&P 500 2H Chart and Trading Levels.
